marks.text: This demo runs on a third-party custom site that simulates the FIFA 2026 website, an external site to the Salesforce org.
It shows how to add a custom Quit button that lets a user cleanly end the Iris agent chat, without changing the out-of-the-box M-I-A-W component.
Everything runs here on the external page. We'll open the agent in full-page mode, start a conversation, ask for today's schedule, and then quit back to this home page.
So, on the home page, we click the chat widget to start a chat with the Iris agent in the U-A-T environment.
Iris opens in full-page mode, and the pre-chat form loads. We fill in our details and start the conversation.
Iris greets us with a personalized welcome.

One thing to notice: this bottom bar, the message box and the Quit button, is not part of the M-I-A-W chat window. It is a component of the custom site that talks to the Iris chat through the Salesforce Embedded Service S-D-K.
We ask Iris for today's match schedule.
Iris replies with the full match schedule for today.
Iris responds with a short feedback survey.
Now the key step: we click the custom Quit button in the bottom bar.
The session ends and the UI switches back to the home page, with no change to the M-I-A-W component.
Clicking the Iris U-A-T widget again immediately starts a brand-new conversation. The pre-chat form reappears.
And that concludes our quick demo on how to support an easy exit from the conversation.
